data.current_description:
Returns the available fields for creating orders, including core fields
and custom fields configured by the tenant.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the schema of available fields for order creation, including both core fields and custom fields configured by the tenant. Returns a list of field definitions with metadata such as field name, type, and validation requirements. Use this operation to discover which fields are required or optional when creating orders.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks clarity about what the response contains and how AI agents should use this information. Added details about the response structure (field definitions with metadata), field properties (name, type, validation), and the operation's purpose in the order creation workflow to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'createOrder' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary and description are too vague. Summary doesn't indicate the action clearly. Description lacks critical details about required vs. optional inputs, what the response contains, and side effects (webhook triggers, state changes). Enhanced version specifies input requirements, response structure, and side effects to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation and what to expect afterward.

data.current_description: Create a new order with optional custom field values.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new order with custom fields
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new order in the TitleTrackr system with required core fields and optional custom field values. Accepts an order object containing order details and any custom field values configured for the tenant. Returns the created order object with a unique order ID and timestamp. This operation creates a persistent record and may trigger the 'order.created' webhook event for subscribed integrations.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too vague. Summary doesn't indicate the action clearly. Description lacks critical details about required vs. optional inputs, what the response contains, and side effects (webhook triggers, state changes). Enhanced version specifies input requirements, response structure, and side effects to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation and what to expect afterward.

message: Operation 'exchangeToken' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent interpretation. Added clarity on the two distinct flows (authorization code vs. refresh token), what inputs are required for each, what the response contains, and how to use the returned token. This helps agents understand when and how to invoke this operation.

data.current_description: Exchange an authorization code for an access token, or refresh an existing token.
data.description_suggestion: Exchange an authorization code for an access token, or refresh an existing access token using a refresh token. Accepts either an authorization code (from the authorization flow) or a refresh token. Returns a TokenResponse containing the new access token, token type, expiration time, and refresh token. The returned access token should be used in subsequent API requests as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent interpretation. Added clarity on the two distinct flows (authorization code vs. refresh token), what inputs are required for each, what the response contains, and how to use the returned token. This helps agents understand when and how to invoke this operation.

message: Operation 'subscribeWebhook' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks specificity about required inputs (eventType values, hook URL structure) and outputs (subscription object details). Added clarity on supported event types, request body requirements, response structure, and the side effect of creating an active subscription. This enables AI agents to properly construct requests and understand the operation's effects.

data.current_description:
Subscribe to receive webhook notifications for a specific event type.
When the event occurs, TitleTrackr will send an HTTP POST request to your hook URL.
data.description_suggestion: Subscribe to receive webhook notifications for a specific event type. Requires the eventType path parameter (e.g., 'order.created', 'abstract.created', 'project.created') and a hook URL in the request body. When the specified event occurs, TitleTrackr will send an HTTP POST request containing event data to your hook URL. Returns a WebhookSubscription object containing the subscription ID and details. This operation creates a new active subscription for the current tenant.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about required inputs (eventType values, hook URL structure) and outputs (subscription object details). Added clarity on supported event types, request body requirements, response structure, and the side effect of creating an active subscription. This enables AI agents to properly construct requests and understand the operation's effects.

message: Operation 'unsubscribeWebhook' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too minimal. Added details about the required eventType parameter, the side effect of deactivating notifications, what the response indicates, and the permanence of the action. This helps AI agents understand what inputs are needed and what state changes occur.

data.description_suggestion: Remove an active webhook subscription for a specific event type. Requires the eventType path parameter to identify which event subscription to remove. Upon successful removal, the webhook subscription is deactivated and no further notifications will be sent for that event type. Returns a confirmation response. This operation permanently removes the subscription for the current tenant.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added details about the required eventType parameter, the side effect of deactivating notifications, what the response indicates, and the permanence of the action. This helps AI agents understand what inputs are needed and what state changes occur.

data.current_description:
Returns the 3 most recent orders for the tenant.
Used by Zapier for trigger testing and sample data.
data.description_suggestion: Returns the 3 most recent orders for the current tenant. This endpoint is designed for Zapier trigger testing and sample data retrieval during integration setup. Returns an array of Order objects containing order details such as ID, status, creation date, and associated project information. Useful for AI agents to understand the structure of order data before processing real webhook events.
